arts.ttl: Europa League | Elfsborg v Roma: Match Preview
arts.sum: The picturesque Boras Arena in Sweden will play host to an intriguing Europa League clash on Thursday evening, as IF Elfsborg welcome AS Roma in a battle between two sides seeking their first win of the group stage. While the hosts aim to end a 46-year wait for a home victory in major European competition, the Italian visitors look to improve their disappointing away record on the continent. Elfsborg’s return to the Europa League group stage after a decade-long absence has been hard-earned. Oscar Hiljemark’s men navigated four qualifying rounds, overcoming the likes of Pafos, Sheriff Tiraspol, Rijeka and Molde The post Europa League | Elfsborg v Roma: Match Preview appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Roma director Ghisolfi: “We want a club that is not dependent on coaches or directors”
arts.sum: Roma’s new sporting director Florent Ghisolfi has given his first interview since joining the club from Nice, sharing insights on the Friedkins’ vision, upcoming changes, and key player situations. Speaking with La Gazzetta dello Sport (via Calciomercato) Ghisolfi emphasized the owners’ ambitions, stating, “The Friedkins want a great team in the new stadium.” He also addressed rumors about the potential Everton purchase, asserting, “The acquisition of Everton? Nothing will change for Roma.” The club is focusing on building stability and expanding its management structure. Ghisolfi revealed, “We want a club that is not dependent on coaches or directors. arts.ttl: Roma face potential €34 million fine from Italian financial police
arts.sum: Roma are facing a potential hefty fine from the Italian tax authorities following an investigation by the Guardia di Finanza. Calciomercato report that the financial police have scrutinized the club’s financial records from 2016 to 2021, issuing a report alleging “unfaithful declaration” in their accounting practices. The club, owned by Dan and Ryan Friedkin, has so far contested these allegations and refused to settle. The potential fine could range from €17 million to a maximum of €34 million , representing administrative sanctions that Roma might have to pay if it’s confirmed they failed to pay €19 million in corporate income The post Roma face potential €34 million fine from Italian financial police appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Roma Begin Haggling to Extend the Contract of Pisilli
arts.sum: The breakout performances of Niccolò Pisilli should soon turn into a contract extension since he’s not tied to Roma long-term.
